_comment: Conservative destructive-command baseline shipped by Tandem. Per Anthropic's guidance to commit permissions.deny rules in .claude/settings.json — see https://claude.com/blog/how-claude-code-works-in-large-codebases-best-practices-and-where-to-start and https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings . Permissions MERGE across scopes (user + project + local), so these project rules ADD to — never weaken — a user's own deny list; deny is evaluated before ask before allow, first match wins. Defaults dated 2026-05-23: destructive-only (irreversible filesystem / history / publish / secret operations). Trim a rule only on near-miss evidence that it blocks a routine command; add project-specific rules under permissions.deny via the BOOTSTRAP discovery question. Bash patterns assume a POSIX shell; PowerShell-host parallels are deferred tech debt. See 90-Standards/CLAUDE-CODE-CONFIG.md §2.1.1 for the three-layer model (ignore vs deny vs hooks).
